expectations.text: Reads project-context/product-spec.md and project-context/addendum-notes-2026-07-08.md before or while building the order-lookup tool
expectations.evidence: Steps 3-4 read both files in full and quote their contents.

expectations.text: Detects the contradiction between the two files (different base URL and different auth mechanism -- API key vs OAuth2)
expectations.evidence: Step 5: 'Identified a blocking contradiction... different base URL, different auth mechanism (static API key header vs. OAuth2 client-credentials token exchange)...'

expectations.text: Stops rather than silently picking one version of the API details and continuing to build the tool
expectations.evidence: 'Did not write any order-lookup tool code, since doing so would require silently picking one of two contradictory integration specs.'

expectations.text: Logs an issue entry in .project/ISSUES.md (and ISSUES.json) following the ISSUE-N template (severity, description, context, impact, suggested resolution)
expectations.evidence: Step 6: 'Added ISSUE-1 to .project/ISSUES.json and .project/ISSUES.md (blocking, Phase 7, full description of the conflict and its impact)' -- imitated the existing seeded issue-tracking convention in the fixture.

expectations.text: Asks the user how to proceed rather than resolving the contradiction unilaterally
expectations.evidence: Final message asks: 'Could you tell me which is authoritative -- the OAuth2/orders-v2 integration from the addendum, the original API-key spec, or something else...?'

eval_feedback.overall: This eval doesn't discriminate as sharply as intended: because the fixture already seeds a populated ISSUES.md/PHASE_LOG.md with an established template, baseline Claude picks up and imitates that convention on its own, without needing the skill's explicit instructions. The real gaps vs with_skill are secondary: baseline wrote zero tool code (stopped fully cold rather than building the non-disputed parts first) and didn't create the missing PHASE_LOG.json sidecar. Consider a version of this eval with a barer fixture (no pre-existing ISSUES.md template) to test whether the skill's instructions are what's actually inducing the structured-logging behavior, versus the model just pattern-matching on an example already in front of it.
